─[1]-Project──
lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──
Rules Skills Agents Fieldnotes Runbooks
─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Review Findings Remediation ]─ ┐

Review Findings Remediation

Six independent external reviews assessed all three repos (lore, create-lore, lore-docs) plus the live docs site. All reviews were positive on architecture and code quality. The actionable findings are release/ops maturity gaps, code hardening opportunities, and documentation depth — not design flaws.

This plan covers items that are net-new from the reviews. Items already tracked in the Launch Content and Distribution plan (README overhaul, screenshots, demo video) are not duplicated here.

Goal

Close the release hygiene, governance, documentation, CI, and code hardening gaps identified by reviewers before public launch.

Scope

0. Showstopper: Version Tag Alignment — COMPLETE

create-lore does git clone --branch v${pkg.version} (v0.8.1), but the lore repo only has a v0.3.0 tag. npx create-lore is broken for end users.

Root cause: fully manual release with no guardrails. Version bumps in package.json and npm publish happen independently of git tags. Tags get forgotten.

Permanent fix: tag-driven releases via GitHub Actions.

Permanent fix deployed (2026-02-19):

  • lore/.github/workflows/release.yml — triggered on v* tag push, runs full test suite, verifies tag matches package.json, creates GitHub Release
  • create-lore/.github/workflows/release.yml — triggered on v* tag push, runs tests, verifies matching lore tag exists before npm publish, creates GitHub Release
  • lore/scripts/release.sh — coordinating script: validates both repos, tags lore first, then create-lore. Both workflows fire automatically.
  • lore/scripts/bump-version.sh — single command to update all version references across both repos
  • lore/scripts/check-version-sync.sh — expanded to catch package-lock.json and SECURITY.md drift

Key guardrails:

  • No tag → no publish (tag is the trigger, not a manual step)
  • create-lore release workflow checks git ls-remote for matching lore tag before publishing
  • Release script enforces: clean working tree, on main, up to date with remote, versions match across repos
  • NPM_TOKEN granular access token configured in create-lore repo settings

Repo: create-lore + lore

  • Add CI check that validates tag existence before npm publish (prevent recurrence)
  • Tag lore repo at current HEAD with version matching create-lore’s package.json
  • Configure NPM_TOKEN secret in create-lore GitHub repo settings
  • Commit and push release workflows to both repos
  • Both repos tagged v0.8.1, GitHub Releases created, lore release workflow green
  • Verify npx create-lore test-project succeeds end-to-end on a machine with github.com access (DNS blocked in current env)

1. Release Hygiene — COMPLETE

Repo: lore + create-lore

  • Fix package-lock.json version drift (0.7.0 → 0.8.1)
  • Update SECURITY.md supported versions table (0.7.x → 0.8.x)
  • Align create-lore GitHub tags/releases with npm publish cadence (tag-driven release workflow)
  • bump-version.sh and check-version-sync.sh now cover create-lore/SECURITY.md (prevented recurring drift)
  • CONTRIBUTING.md: removed hardcoded test count to prevent drift
  • Document release workflow in runbook (cross-ref with launch plan Section 0) — scripts/release.sh is the operator entry point

2. Governance Files in Satellite Repos — COMPLETE

Both reviewers noted that lore-docs and create-lore lack standard open-source process files. The core lore repo already has these.

Repo: lore-docs + create-lore

  • Add CONTRIBUTING.md to lore-docs
  • Add CONTRIBUTING.md to create-lore
  • Add SECURITY.md to lore-docs
  • Add SECURITY.md to create-lore
  • Add issue templates (bug report, feature request) to lore-docs
  • Add issue templates (bug report, feature request) to create-lore

3. Documentation Gaps — COMPLETE

Items not covered by the existing README overhaul in the launch plan.

Repo: lore-docs

  • Add troubleshooting/FAQ page to docs site
  • Add changelog page to docs site (v0.1.0 through v0.8.1)

Repo: create-lore

  • Rewrite create-lore README to explain what Lore is for newcomers
  • Add explicit failure messaging when git clone fails (tag missing, network error)

4. Docs CI Hardening — COMPLETE

lore-docs has deploy-only automation. No validation runs before publish, so regressions can slip through.

Repo: lore-docs

  • Add mkdocs build --strict to CI (catches broken links, missing pages)
  • Add markdown lint check to CI (markdownlint-cli2 with .markdownlint.json config)
  • Run validation before deploy step (validate job gates deploy job)
  • Add pull_request trigger so validation runs on PRs, not just push to main
  • Supply chain hardening: all GitHub Actions pinned by commit SHA across all 3 repos

5. Hook Token Cost Measurement (PRIORITY)

Both reviewers flagged that the cumulative context window cost of hooks in long sessions is unmeasured. The hook-logger infrastructure exists but no analysis has been published.

Reprioritized for launch readiness — this now feeds the Phase 4 evidence-first sequence directly (proof assets, FAQ, and competitive positioning).

Repo: lore

  • Measure token cost of all hook injections across a representative session (10+ prompts)
  • Document findings — total tokens injected, percentage of typical context window consumed
  • Publish summary in docs (architecture or FAQ section) for transparency
  • If cost exceeds 5% of context window, evaluate optimization options

6. Code Hardening — MOSTLY COMPLETE

From reviews 3 and 4. Improve robustness of parsing, scripts, and scaffolder input handling.

Repo: lore

  • Extract shared lib/frontmatter.js module — zero-dependency parser with CRLF, quoted values, inline comments, edge cases (13 tests)
  • Refactor lib/banner.js to use shared frontmatter module (removed all inline regex parsing)
  • Add c8 code coverage reporting — npm run test:coverage (97.5% statements, 100% functions)
  • Banner.js unit tests — 41 tests covering all 6 exported functions (coverage 61% → 99%)
  • Fix “under 100 lines” and “~1,200 lines” claims in production-readiness.md
  • Break lore-link.sh into smaller composable scripts (flagged as monolithic, maintenance risk)
  • Handle malformed .lore-config gracefully in scaffolder (try/catch on JSON.parse)

Repo: create-lore

  • Validate project names for shell-hostile characters — validates resolved basename, catches ;|$& etc.
  • Clarify dual actions/checkout in workflows (comments explain: first = this repo, second = lore template)
  • create-lore: added ESLint + Prettier with CI gate for parity with lore repo

7. CI Expansion — COMPLETE

Repo: create-lore

  • Add E2E CI job: scaffold project → run validate-consistency.sh on result (runs on all 3 OSes)
  • Add windows-latest to test and release CI matrices (validates rsync→cp migration)

Repo: lore

  • Add windows-latest to test and release CI matrices (ShellCheck and nav check gated to non-Windows)
  • Add npm audit --omit=dev to CI
  • Windows CI stabilization: .gitattributes eol=lf, node --test auto-discovery, process.argv for paths in bash→Node, lore-link test skips, CRLF normalization

8. Documentation — Launch Content — COMPLETE

Items that feed directly into launch positioning. Cross-referenced with launch plan but owned here because they came from reviews. Reviews 5 and 6 reinforced the need for production readiness docs and added new items.

Repo: lore-docs

  • Add “Production Readiness” page — stability policy, security/trust model, supply chain, known limitations, non-goals, CI coverage, upgrade policy
  • Add “When to Use Lore” decision guide (good/poor fit, team workflows, comparison with alternatives, migration paths)

Assessed and deferred (not needed for launch):

  • API reference for lib/ modules — internal implementation details; modules are small, well-commented, self-documenting. Per docs conventions rule 4.
  • Migration guide — only one published version; nothing to migrate from yet. Create when v0.9.0 ships.
  • Agent authoring walkthrough — /lore-create-skill and /lore-create-agent already guide users through creation. Audited: non-lore-* skills/agents flow correctly through registries, platform sync, banner, validation, and framework sync (never touched). A walkthrough would restate what the tooling handles.
  • Versioned docs — infrastructure work, deferred to post-launch.

9. Safe Mode / Restricted Execution (deferred — feature request)

Review 5 suggested a “safe mode” for regulated environments where hook execution could be restricted or sandboxed. This is a feature request, not a documentation or hygiene gap. Deferred to post-launch backlog.

  • Evaluate restricted execution profile (disable specific hooks, read-only mode, audit logging)
  • If pursued, document in Production Readiness page

10. Review Reconciliation (Reviews 7–8)

Three items from reconciling the two most recent external reviews.

Repo: lore-docs

  • First session walkthrough — guided end-to-end tutorial from npx create-lore through first capture (Review 7’s strongest docs gap)
  • Platform compatibility matrix — what’s stable vs experimental per platform and version (Review 8)

Repo: lore + lore-docs

  • Docs sync policy — document or automate the boundary between lore/docs/ (instance template) and lore-docs/docs/ (public site) to prevent drift (Review 8)

11. Supply Chain & CI Hardening (Reviews 9–10) — COMPLETE

All repos

  • Pin all GitHub Actions by commit SHA (23 action refs across 3 repos)
  • Switch CI from npm install to npm ci for deterministic builds

Repo: create-lore

  • Add ESLint + Prettier CI gate for lint/format parity with lore
  • Enable npm publish --provenance with id-token: write permission
  • Pin ESLint to v9 for Node 18 compatibility
  • Add .prettierignore and ESLint ignores for lore checkout directory

12. Marketing Language & Governance (Reviews 9–10) — COMPLETE

Repo: lore

  • README: “gets smarter” → “starts with full context”
  • Added CODEOWNERS file
  • Framework instructions: added lore-link/IDE workflow to repo boundary section
  • Delegation instructions: agent rules, conventions, skills now REQUIRED

Repo: create-lore

  • Added CODEOWNERS file

Repo: lore-docs

  • “Self-Learning” → “Knowledge Capture” on index page
  • “learns their patterns” → “captures their patterns” in when-to-use guide
  • “pick up where they left off” → “have persistent context from day one”
  • “The One Rule” reframed as “Two Workflows” — CLI and IDE are equal
  • Cross-repo diagram: neutral hub label, link to both workflows
  • Added CODEOWNERS file

Repo: lore (source template)

  • docs/guides/working-across-repos.md: “The One Rule” → “Two Workflows”, expanded lore-link section

Repo: lorehq (instance)

  • README: “Self-Learning” → “Persistent Context”
  • docs/guides/working-across-repos.md: “The One Rule” → “Two Workflows”, full IDE workflow section
  • docs/guides/commands.md: added /lore-link to slash commands table
  • docs/guides/platform-support.md: updated feature matrix, hook directories, linked repos references guide

Deliverables

  • Working npx create-lore (version tags aligned across repos)
  • SECURITY.md current in all repos
  • CONTRIBUTING.md and issue templates in lore-docs and create-lore
  • Troubleshooting/FAQ page on docs site
  • Changelog page on docs site
  • Rewritten create-lore README
  • Docs CI pipeline with build + lint validation
  • Published hook token cost analysis
  • Hardened frontmatter parsing and scaffolder input validation
  • E2E CI job for create-lore
  • Production readiness page (with security/trust model and stability policy)
  • Decision guide (When to Use Lore page)
  • GitHub Actions pinned by SHA, npm provenance enabled
  • Marketing language audit — all overpromising claims fixed
  • CODEOWNERS in all repos

Success Criteria

  • All three repos have CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, and issue templates
  • mkdocs build --strict passes in lore-docs CI
  • No version mismatches across package.json, package-lock.json, SECURITY.md, and git tags
  • npx create-lore test-project succeeds on clean Ubuntu and macOS
  • Hook token cost is documented with real measurements
  • lib/banner.js frontmatter parsing handles malformed input without crash
  • create-lore rejects invalid project names
  • E2E scaffolder CI job passes

Sequencing

Done:

  • Sections 0-4 — completed 2026-02-19, enhanced 2026-02-20 (version sync prevention, PR-trigger CI)
  • Section 6 — code hardening mostly complete (frontmatter module, scaffolder validation, workflow comments, c8 coverage, banner tests, docs accuracy). Remaining: lore-link.sh decomposition.
  • Section 7 — CI expansion complete (E2E job, Windows matrix, npm audit)
  • Section 8 — launch content docs complete (Production Readiness, When to Use Lore). Remaining items assessed and deferred.
  • Section 11 — supply chain and CI hardening complete (2026-02-20)
  • Section 12 — marketing language, governance, and guide fixes complete (2026-02-20)

Remaining before launch (priority order):

  • P0: Section 5 — token cost measurement and published transparency report
  • P1: Section 10 — review reconciliation: first session walkthrough, compatibility matrix, docs sync policy
  • P2: Section 6 — lore-link.sh decomposition (maintenance quality)

Deferred to post-launch:

  • Section 9 — safe mode / restricted execution (feature request)
  • Versioned docs, migration guide (premature with one published version)

Cross-references:

  • Launch plan Section 0 (pre-launch gates) — release cadence runbook overlaps with Section 1 here
  • Launch plan Section 1 (proof assets) — token cost data is a proof asset
  • Launch plan Section 2 (README overhaul) — create-lore README rewrite here is complementary, not duplicate
  • Launch plan Section 5 (competitive differentiation) — “complexity vs value” concern maps to messaging
  • Review 5 — security/trust model and stability policy folded into Section 8 Production Readiness page
  • Review 6 — API reference, migration guide, agent authoring walkthrough added to Section 8

Test Plan

# In lore repo
bash scripts/validate-consistency.sh

# In lore-docs repo
mkdocs build --strict

# End-to-end scaffolder test
npx create-lore /tmp/test-project && cd /tmp/test-project && bash scripts/validate-consistency.sh
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
